text: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ λέγοντες·
translation: When evening had come, his disciples came to him, saying, “This place is deserted, and the hour is already late. Send the multitudes away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves food.” (Matthew 14:15)

text: λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ὑμεῖς δὲ τίνα με λέγετε εἶναι;
translation: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” (Matthew 16:15)

text: τότε ἔρχεται πρὸς τοὺς μαθητὰς καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς·
translation: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners. (Matthew 26:45)

text: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί θέλετε ποιήσω ὑμῖν;
translation: He said to them, “What do you want me to do for you?” (Mark 10:36)

text: αὐτὸς Δαυὶδ εἶπεν ἐν τῷ πνεύματι τῷ ἁγίῳ·
translation: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’ (Mark 12:36)

text: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ πατὴρ πρὸς τοὺς δούλους αὐτοῦ·
translation: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet. (Luke 15:22)

text: Σὺ τίς εἶ; εἰδότες ὅτι ὁ κύριός ἐστιν.
translation: Jesus said to them, “Come and eat breakfast!” None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord. (John 21:12)

text: σταθήσεται δέ, δυνατεῖ γὰρ ὁ κύριος στῆσαι αὐτόν.
translation: Who are you who judge another’s servant? To his own lord he stands or falls. Yes, he will be made to stand, for God has power to make him stand. (Romans 14:4)

text: ὁ δὲ κολλώμενος τῷ κυρίῳ ἓν πνεῦμά ἐστιν.
translation: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit. (1 Corinthians 6:17)

text: Αἱ γυναῖκες τοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνδράσιν ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ,
translation: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord. (Ephesians 5:22)

text: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: “But his lord answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant. You knew that I reap where I didn’t sow, and gather where I didn’t scatter. (Matthew 25:26)

text: καὶ γὰρ χεὶρ κυρίου ἦν μετ’ αὐτοῦ.
translation: All who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, “What then will this child be?” The hand of the Lord was with him. (Luke 1:66)

text: Εἶπεν δὲ ὁ ἀρχιερεύς· Εἰ ταῦτα οὕτως ἔχει;
translation: The high priest said, “Are these things so?” (Acts 7:1)

text: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ἔχετε πίστιν θεοῦ·
translation: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God. (Mark 11:22)

text: Εἶπεν δέ· Ἄνθρωπός τις εἶχεν δύο υἱούς.
translation: He said, “A certain man had two sons. (Luke 15:11)

text: καὶ οὐ θέλετε ἐλθεῖν πρός με ἵνα ζωὴν ἔχητε.
translation: Yet you will not come to me, that you may have life. (John 5:40)

text: διὰ τοῦτο ὁ παραδούς μέ σοι μείζονα ἁμαρτίαν ἔχει.
translation: Jesus answered, “You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.” (John 19:11)

text: ἀπεκρίθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς· Οὐκ ἔχομεν βασιλέα εἰ μὴ Καίσαρα.
translation: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!” (John 19:15)

text: οὐ γὰρ ᾔδει τί ἀποκριθῇ, ἔκφοβοι γὰρ ἐγένοντο.
translation: For he didn’t know what to say, for they were very afraid. (Mark 9:6)

text: (αὕτη ἀπογραφὴ πρώτη ἐγένετο ἡγεμονεύοντος τῆς Συρίας Κυρηνίου·)
translation: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria. (Luke 2:2)

text: Ἐγένετο ἄνθρωπος ἀπεσταλμένος παρὰ θεοῦ, ὄνομα αὐτῷ Ἰωάννης·
translation: There came a man sent from God, whose name was John. (John 1:6)

text: καὶ ἐγένοντο φωναὶ μεγάλαι ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ λέγοντες·
translation: The seventh angel sounded, and great voices in heaven followed, saying, “The kingdom of the world has become the Kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ. He will reign forever and ever!” (Revelation 11:15)

text: καὶ ἦλθον ἰδεῖν τί ἐστιν τὸ γεγονός.
translation: Those who fed the pigs fled, and told it in the city and in the country. The people came to see what it was that had happened. (Mark 5:14)

text: καὶ Πιλᾶτος ἐπέκρινεν γενέσθαι τὸ αἴτημα αὐτῶν·
translation: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done. (Luke 23:24)

text: Οὐκ ἔρχεται ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ μετὰ παρατηρήσεως,
translation: Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation; (Luke 17:20)

text: πολλοὶ γὰρ ἐλεύσονται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου λέγοντες·
translation: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them. (Luke 21:8)

text: οὗτος ἦλθεν πρὸς αὐτὸν νυκτὸς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: He came to Jesus by night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.” (John 3:2)

text: καὶ ἦλθον πρὸς τὸν Ἰωάννην καὶ εἶπαν αὐτῷ·
translation: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, he baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.” (John 3:26)

text: ὁ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἐρχόμενος ἐπάνω πάντων ἐστίν·
translation: “He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all. (John 3:31)

text: ἐξῆλθον ἐκ τῆς πόλεως καὶ ἤρχοντο πρὸς αὐτόν.
translation: They went out of the city, and were coming to him. (John 4:30)

text: εἰ ταῦτα οἴδατε, μακάριοί ἐστε ἐὰν ποιῆτε αὐτά.
translation: If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them. (John 13:17)

text: Οὐ δύναμαι ἐγὼ ποιεῖν ἀπ’ ἐμαυτοῦ οὐδέν·
translation: I can of myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge; and my judgment is righteous, because I don’t seek my own will, but the will of my Father who sent me. (John 5:30)

text: εἰπόντες τῷ Ἀαρών· Ποίησον ἡμῖν θεοὺς οἳ προπορεύσονται ἡμῶν·
translation: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’ (Acts 7:40)

text: οὐχὶ ἡ χείρ μου ἐποίησεν ταῦτα πάντα;
translation: Didn’t my hand make all these things?’ (Acts 7:50)

text: ταῦτα ἔδει ποιῆσαι κἀκεῖνα μὴ ἀφιέναι.
translation: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone. (Matthew 23:23)

text: κατὰ τὰ αὐτὰ γὰρ ἐποίουν τοῖς προφήταις οἱ πατέρες αὐτῶν.
translation: Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same thing to the prophets. (Luke 6:23)

text: ἢ τί δώσει ἄνθρωπος ἀντάλλαγμα τῆς ψυχῆς αὐτοῦ;
translation: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and forfeits his life? Or what will a man give in exchange for his life? (Matthew 16:26)

text: τί γὰρ δοῖ ἄνθρωπος ἀντάλλαγμα τῆς ψυχῆς αὐτοῦ;
translation: For what will a man give in exchange for his life? (Mark 8:37)

text: καὶ ἰδοὺ ἄνθρωπός τις ἦν ὑδρωπικὸς ἔμπροσθεν αὐτοῦ.
translation: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him. (Luke 14:2)

text: θέλω δὲ πάντας ἀνθρώπους εἶναι ὡς καὶ ἐμαυτόν·
translation: Yet I wish that all men were like me. However, each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind. (1 Corinthians 7:7)

text: Τίνα λέγουσιν οἱ ἄνθρωποι εἶναι τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου;
translation: Now when Jesus came into the parts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, “Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?” (Matthew 16:13)

text: τίς ἐστιν οὗτος ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου;
translation: The multitude answered him, “We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever. How do you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?” (John 12:34)

text: ὤφθη δὲ αὐτῷ ἄγγελος ἀπ’ οὐρανοῦ ἐνισχύων αὐτόν.
translation: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him. (Luke 22:43)

text: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ Πέτρος ἀπεκρίνατο πρὸς τὸν λαόν·
translation: When Peter saw it, he responded to the people, “You men of Israel, why do you marvel at this man? Why do you fasten your eyes on us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made him walk? (Acts 3:12)

text: Ἑωράκαμεν τὸν κύριον. ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς·
translation: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.” (John 20:25)

text: καὶ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἰδὼν αὐτὸν ὅτι νουνεχῶς ἀπεκρίθη εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from God’s Kingdom.” No one dared ask him any question after that. (Mark 12:34)

text: ἀλλ’ εἶπον ὑμῖν ὅτι καὶ ἑωράκατέ με καὶ οὐ πιστεύετε.
translation: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe. (John 6:36)

text: ἐραύνησον καὶ ἴδε ὅτι ἐκ τῆς Γαλιλαίας προφήτης οὐκ ἐγείρεται.
translation: They answered him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search and see that no prophet has arisen out of Galilee.” (John 7:52)

text: Εἶπαν δὲ οἱ ἀκούσαντες· Καὶ τίς δύναται σωθῆναι;
translation: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?” (Luke 18:26)

text: Ἀκούοντος δὲ παντὸς τοῦ λαοῦ εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς·
translation: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples, (Luke 20:45)

text: Καὶ ἤκουσα φωνὴν μεγάλην ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ λέγουσαν·
translation: I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying, “Now the salvation, the power, and the Kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ has come; for the accuser of our brothers has been thrown down, who accuses them before our God day and night. (Revelation 12:10)

text: Καὶ ἤκουσα ἄλλην φωνὴν ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ λέγουσαν·
translation: I heard another voice from heaven, saying, “Come out of her, my people, that you have no participation in her sins, and that you don’t receive of her plagues, (Revelation 18:4)

text: καὶ εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Ἀκούεις τί οὗτοι λέγουσιν;
translation: and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes. Did you never read, ‘Out of the mouth of children and nursing babies, you have perfected praise’?” (Matthew 21:16)

text: αὐτοῦ ἀκούσεσθε κατὰ πάντα ὅσα ἂν λαλήσῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς.
translation: For Moses indeed said to the fathers, ‘The Lord God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him in all things whatever he says to you. (Acts 3:22)

text: καὶ πολλῷ πλείους ἐπίστευσαν διὰ τὸν λόγον αὐτοῦ,
translation: Many more believed because of his word. (John 4:41)

text: πολλοὶ ἐροῦσίν μοι ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ·
translation: Many will tell me in that day, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?’ (Matthew 7:22)

text: ταῦτα αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος πολλοὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτόν.
translation: As he spoke these things, many believed in him. (John 8:30)

text: καὶ οἱ πολλοὶ ἀκούοντες ἐξεπλήσσοντο λέγοντες·
translation: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands? (Mark 6:2)

text: ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου δὲ πολλοὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτόν, καὶ ἔλεγον·
translation: But of the multitude, many believed in him. They said, “When the Christ comes, he won’t do more signs than those which this man has done, will he?” (John 7:31)

text: Σίμων Ἰωάννου, ἀγαπᾷς με πλέον τούτων;
translation: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Feed my lambs.” (John 21:15)

text: ἐκεῖνον λαβὼν δὸς αὐτοῖς ἀντὶ ἐμοῦ καὶ σοῦ.
translation: But, lest we cause them to stumble, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take up the first fish that comes up. When you have opened its mouth, you will find a stater coin. Take that, and give it to them for me and you.” (Matthew 17:27)

text: οὐ καθὼς ὁ κόσμος δίδωσιν ἐγὼ δίδωμι ὑμῖν.
translation: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, I give to you. Don’t let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful. (John 14:27)

text: Τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ σῶμά μου [τὸ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν διδόμενον·
translation: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.” (Luke 22:19)

text: ἄρα ἕκαστος ἡμῶν περὶ ἑαυτοῦ λόγον δώσει.
translation: So then each one of us will give account of himself to God. (Romans 14:12)

text: νῦν ἔγνωκαν ὅτι πάντα ὅσα δέδωκάς μοι παρὰ σοῦ εἰσιν·
translation: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you, (John 17:7)

text: οὐ δύναμαι ἀναστὰς δοῦναί σοι.
translation: and he from within will answer and say, ‘Don’t bother me. The door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed. I can’t get up and give it to you’? (Luke 11:7)

text: Μετὰ δέ τινας ἡμέρας εἶπεν πρὸς Βαρναβᾶν Παῦλος·
translation: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Let’s return now and visit our brothers in every city in which we proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing.” (Acts 15:36)

text: αὐτῷ ἡ δόξα καὶ νῦν καὶ εἰς ἡμέραν αἰῶνος.
translation: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and forever. Amen. (2 Peter 3:18)

text: τοῦτο δὲ ἐποίει ἐπὶ πολλὰς ἡμέρας.
translation: She was doing this for many days. But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” It came out that very hour. (Acts 16:18)

text: ὁ λόγος ὃν ἐλάλησα ἐκεῖνος κρινεῖ αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ·
translation: He who rejects me, and doesn’t receive my sayings, has one who judges him. The word that I spoke will judge him in the last day. (John 12:48)

text: Καὶ εἰσελθὼν πάλιν εἰς Καφαρναοὺμ δι’ ἡμερῶν ἠκούσθη ὅτι ἐν οἴκῳ ἐστίν·
translation: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was at home. (Mark 2:1)

text: τοῦτον ὁ θεὸς ἤγειρεν τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτὸν ἐμφανῆ γενέσθαι,
translation: God raised him up the third day and gave him to be revealed, (Acts 10:40)

text: εἰ δὲ πνεῦμα ἐλάλησεν αὐτῷ ἢ ἄγγελος—
translation: A great clamor arose, and some of the scribes of the Pharisees’ part stood up, and contended, saying, “We find no evil in this man. But if a spirit or angel has spoken to him, let’s not fight against God!” (Acts 23:9)

text: Κύριος Ἰησοῦς εἰ μὴ ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ.
translation: Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit. (1 Corinthians 12:3)

text: καὶ ἀναστενάξας τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ λέγει·
translation: He sighed deeply in his spirit and said, “Why does this generation seek a sign? Most certainly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.” (Mark 8:12)

text: καὶ τοῦτο εἰπὼν ἐνεφύσησεν καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Λάβετε πνεῦμα ἅγιον·
translation: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit! (John 20:22)

text: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματος ὑμῶν.
translation: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen. (Philippians 4:23)

text: Ὁ κύριος μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματός σου. ἡ χάρις μεθ’ ὑμῶν.
translation: The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen. (2 Timothy 4:22)

text: οὐδὲ γὰρ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ ἐπίστευον εἰς αὐτόν.
translation: For even his brothers didn’t believe in him. (John 7:5)

text: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Ἀναστήσεται ὁ ἀδελφός σου.
translation: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.” (John 11:23)

text: ὃς δ’ ἂν εἴπῃ τῷ ἀδελφῷ αὐτοῦ·
translation: But I tell you that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause will be in danger of the judgment. Whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca!’ will be in danger of the council. Whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of Gehenna. (Matthew 5:22)

text: εἶπον οὖν πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ·
translation: His brothers therefore said to him, “Depart from here and go into Judea, that your disciples also may see your works which you do. (John 7:3)

text: πορεύου δὲ πρὸς τοὺς ἀδελφούς μου καὶ εἰπὲ αὐτοῖς·
translation: Jesus said to her, “Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’” (John 20:17)

text: Ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί, ἐγὼ Φαρισαῖός εἰμι, υἱὸς Φαρισαίων·
translation: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, “Men and brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged!” (Acts 23:6)

text: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ εἷς ὀνόματι Κλεοπᾶς εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν·
translation: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things which have happened there in these days?” (Luke 24:18)

text: καθὼς γέγραπται ὅτι Οὐκ ἔστιν δίκαιος οὐδὲ εἷς,
translation: As it is written, “There is no one righteous; no, not one. (Romans 3:10)

text: πάντες γὰρ ὑμεῖς εἷς ἐστε ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
translation: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus. (Galatians 3:28)

text: ὅτι μιᾷ ὥρᾳ ἠρημώθη ὁ τοσοῦτος πλοῦτος.
translation: For in an hour such great riches are made desolate.’ Every ship master, and everyone who sails anywhere, and mariners, and as many as gain their living by sea, stood far away, (Revelation 18:17)

text: μηδὲ κληθῆτε καθηγηταί, ὅτι καθηγητὴς ὑμῶν ἐστιν εἷς ὁ χριστός·
translation: Neither be called masters, for one is your master, the Christ. (Matthew 23:10)

text: ἐγὼ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἕν ἐσμεν.
translation: I and the Father are one.” (John 10:30)

text: οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ ἐθαμβοῦντο ἐπὶ τοῖς λόγοις αὐτοῦ.
translation: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, “Children, how hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom! (Mark 10:24)

text: ὡς γέγραπται ἐν βίβλῳ λόγων Ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου·
translation: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord. Make his paths straight. (Luke 3:4)

text: ὥστε παρακαλεῖτε ἀλλήλους ἐν τοῖς λόγοις τούτοις.
translation: Therefore comfort one another with these words. (1 Thessalonians 4:18)

text: οὐ γὰρ ἐν λόγῳ ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλ’ ἐν δυνάμει.
translation: For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power. (1 Corinthians 4:20)

text: καὶ ὃς ἐὰν εἴπῃ λόγον κατὰ τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου, ἀφεθήσεται αὐτῷ·
translation: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, either in this age, or in that which is to come. (Matthew 12:32)

text: ἐπίστευσεν ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῷ λόγῳ ὃν εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς καὶ ἐπορεύετο.
translation: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your son lives.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way. (John 4:50)

text: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Ἐγώ εἰμι, ὁ λαλῶν σοι.
translation: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who speaks to you.” (John 4:26)

text: ὁ ἀφ’ ἑαυτοῦ λαλῶν τὴν δόξαν τὴν ἰδίαν ζητεῖ·
translation: He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and no unrighteousness is in him. (John 7:18)

text: Πάλιν οὖν αὐτοῖς ἐλάλησεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγων·
translation: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.” (John 8:12)

text: Ἄγγελος δὲ κυρίου ἐλάλησεν πρὸς Φίλιππον λέγων·
translation: Then an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise, and go toward the south to the way that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza. This is a desert.” (Acts 8:26)

text: Τότε ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐλάλησεν τοῖς ὄχλοις καὶ τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ
translation: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples, (Matthew 23:1)

text: Ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν παρ’ ὑμῖν μένων·
translation: “I have said these things to you while still living with you. (John 14:25)

text: καὶ οὐκ ἐγνώκατε αὐτόν, ἐγὼ δὲ οἶδα αὐτόν·
translation: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him and keep his word. (John 8:55)

text: πιστεύεις, βασιλεῦ Ἀγρίππα, τοῖς προφήταις; οἶδα ὅτι πιστεύεις.
translation: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you believe.” (Acts 26:27)

text: Οὐκ οἴδαμεν. καὶ ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς·
translation: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.” (Mark 11:33)

text: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς· Οὔτε ἐμὲ οἴδατε οὔτε τὸν πατέρα μου·
translation: They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.” (John 8:19)

text: εἰ ἐμὲ ᾔδειτε, καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου ἂν ᾔδειτε.
translation: They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.” (John 8:19)

text: οὐκ οἴδατε ὅτι ἀγγέλους κρινοῦμεν, μήτιγε βιωτικά;
translation: Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life? (1 Corinthians 6:3)

text: εἶπαν οὖν ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ πρὸς ἀλλήλους·
translation: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and, ‘Because I go to the Father’?” (John 16:17)

text: Τότε προσέρχονται αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ Ἰωάννου λέγοντες·
translation: Then John’s disciples came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples don’t fast?” (Matthew 9:14)

text: καὶ προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες·
translation: Then Jesus sent the multitudes away, and went into the house. His disciples came to him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the darnel weeds of the field.” (Matthew 13:36)

text: Τότε ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ·
translation: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me. (Matthew 16:24)

text: καὶ ἀποστέλλει δύο τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς·
translation: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go into the city, and there a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him, (Mark 14:13)

text: ἀπῆλθον οὖν πάλιν πρὸς αὑτοὺς οἱ μαθηταί.
translation: So the disciples went away again to their own homes. (John 20:10)

text: Λάβετε φάγετε, τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ σῶμά μου.
translation: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks for it, and broke it. He gave to the disciples and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.” (Matthew 26:26)

text: Διετίας δὲ πληρωθείσης ἔλαβεν διάδοχον ὁ Φῆλιξ Πόρκιον Φῆστον·
translation: But when two years were fulfilled, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus, and desiring to gain favor with the Jews, Felix left Paul in bonds. (Acts 24:27)

text: ἐὰν ἄλλος ἔλθῃ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τῷ ἰδίῳ, ἐκεῖνον λήμψεσθε.
translation: I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him. (John 5:43)

text: Λάβετε αὐτὸν ὑμεῖς, καὶ κατὰ τὸν νόμον ὑμῶν κρίνατε αὐτόν.
translation: Pilate therefore said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is illegal for us to put anyone to death,” (John 18:31)

text: καὶ λαβὼν ἐνώπιον αὐτῶν ἔφαγεν.
translation: He took them, and ate in front of them. (Luke 24:43)

text: δόξαν παρὰ ἀνθρώπων οὐ λαμβάνω,
translation: I don’t receive glory from men. (John 5:41)

text: Λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐδὲ ἐν τῷ Ἰσραὴλ τοσαύτην πίστιν εὗρον.
translation: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.” (Luke 7:9)

text: Ἀλλ’ ἐρεῖ τις· Σὺ πίστιν ἔχεις κἀγὼ ἔργα ἔχω.
translation: Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I will show you my faith by my works. (James 2:18)

text: Ὦ γύναι, μεγάλη σου ἡ πίστις·
translation: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour. (Matthew 15:28)

text: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Ἀναστὰς πορεύου· ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε.
translation: Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.” (Luke 17:19)

text: Γινώσκετε ἄρα ὅτι οἱ ἐκ πίστεως, οὗτοι υἱοί εἰσιν Ἀβραάμ.
translation: Know therefore that those who are of faith are children of Abraham. (Galatians 3:7)

text: πάντες γὰρ υἱοὶ θεοῦ ἐστε διὰ τῆς πίστεως ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
translation: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus. (Galatians 3:26)

text: ἀλλὰ εἰσὶν ἐξ ὑμῶν τινες οἳ οὐ πιστεύουσιν.
translation: But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him. (John 6:64)

text: περὶ ἁμαρτίας μέν, ὅτι οὐ πιστεύουσιν εἰς ἐμέ·
translation: about sin, because they don’t believe in me; (John 16:9)

text: Ἔλεγεν οὖν ὁ Ἰησοῦς πρὸς τοὺς πεπιστευκότας αὐτῷ Ἰουδαίους·
translation: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples. (John 8:31)

text: διὰ τοῦτο οὐκ ἠδύναντο πιστεύειν ὅτι πάλιν εἶπεν Ἠσαΐας·
translation: For this cause they couldn’t believe, for Isaiah said again: (John 12:39)

text: Διὰ τί οὖν οὐκ ἐπιστεύσατε αὐτῷ;
translation: The baptism of John, where was it from? From heaven or from men?” They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’ (Matthew 21:25)

text: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν οὐ κρίνεται·
translation: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the only born Son of God. (John 3:18)

text: Οὐκ ἔστιν δοῦλος μείζων τοῦ κυρίου αὐτοῦ·
translation: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his lord.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours. (John 15:20)

text: καὶ ἤκουσαν φωνῆς μεγάλης ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ λεγούσης αὐτοῖς·
translation: I heard a loud voice from heaven saying to them, “Come up here!” They went up into heaven in a cloud, and their enemies saw them. (Revelation 11:12)

text: Γνῶθι τὸν κύριον, ὅτι πάντες εἰδήσουσίν με ἀπὸ μικροῦ ἕως μεγάλου αὐτῶν.
translation: They will not teach every man his fellow citizen and every man his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ for all will know me, from their least to their greatest. (Hebrews 8:11)

text: λέγων ἐν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ·
translation: He said with a loud voice, “Fear the Lord, and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship him who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and the springs of waters!” (Revelation 14:7)

text: Μετὰ ταῦτα ἤκουσα ὡς φωνὴν μεγάλην ὄχλου πολλοῦ ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ λεγόντων·
translation: After these things I heard something like a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, “Hallelujah! Salvation, power, and glory belong to our God; (Revelation 19:1)

text: διότι γέγραπται ὅτι Ἅγιοι ἔσεσθε, ὅτι ἐγὼ ἅγιος.
translation: because it is written, “You shall be holy, for I am holy.” (1 Peter 1:16)

text: καὶ ὑμεῖς χρῖσμα ἔχετε ἀπὸ τοῦ ἁγίου καὶ οἴδατε πάντες·
translation: You have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all have knowledge. (1 John 2:20)

text: καὶ ἡμεῖς πεπιστεύκαμεν καὶ ἐγνώκαμεν ὅτι σὺ εἶ ὁ ἅγιος τοῦ θεοῦ.
translation: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.” (John 6:69)

text: Ἀσπάσασθε πάντα ἅγιον ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ. ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ σὺν ἐμοὶ ἀδελφοί.
translation: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you. (Philippians 4:21)

text: μαρτυρεῖ δὲ ἡμῖν καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον, μετὰ γὰρ τὸ εἰρηκέναι·
translation: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying, (Hebrews 10:15)

text: Διό, καθὼς λέγει τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον· Σήμερον ἐὰν τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούσητε,
translation: Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says, “Today if you will hear his voice, (Hebrews 3:7)

text: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Σὺ λέγεις ὅτι βασιλεύς εἰμι.
translation: Pilate therefore said to him, “Are you a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.” (John 18:37)

text: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ Πέτρος εἶπεν τῷ Ἰησοῦ·
translation: Peter answered and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, let’s make three tents here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” (Matthew 17:4)

text: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Πέτρος λέγει τῷ Ἰησοῦ·
translation: Peter answered Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” (Mark 9:5)

text: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς πάλιν ἀποκριθεὶς λέγει αὐτοῖς·
translation: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, “Children, how hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom! (Mark 10:24)

text: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς πρὸς αὐτοὺς εἶπεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς·
translation: Jesus, answering them, said, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him, (Luke 6:3)

text: ἀπεκρίθη δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος καὶ εἶπεν·
translation: Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath and lead him away to water? (Luke 13:15)

text: ἦν δὲ ὄνομα τῷ δούλῳ Μάλχος.
translation: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus. (John 18:10)

text: ἐγὼ ἐλήλυθα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ πατρός μου καὶ οὐ λαμβάνετέ με·
translation: I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him. (John 5:43)

text: Ὡσαννά, εὐλογημένος ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου, καὶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῦ Ἰσραήλ.
translation: they took the branches of the palm trees and went out to meet him, and cried out, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel!” (John 12:13)

text: Οἶδά σου τὰ ἔργα, ὅτι ὄνομα ἔχεις ὅτι ζῇς, καὶ νεκρὸς εἶ.
translation: “And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: “He who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars says these things: “I know your works, that you have a reputation of being alive, but you are dead. (Revelation 3:1)

text: πολλοὶ ἐλεύσονται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου λέγοντες ὅτι Ἐγώ εἰμι, καὶ πολλοὺς πλανήσουσιν.
translation: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and will lead many astray. (Mark 13:6)

text: ἀνθρώποις παραδεδωκόσι τὰς ψυχὰς αὐτῶν ὑπὲρ τοῦ ὀνόματος τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
translation: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ. (Acts 15:26)

text: οὐκ ἔγνωσαν ὅτι τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῖς ἔλεγεν.
translation: They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father. (John 8:27)

text: ἐν τούτῳ γινώσκομεν ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ ἐσμεν·
translation: But God’s love has most certainly been perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know that we are in him: (1 John 2:5)

text: καὶ ταῦτα ποιήσουσιν ὅτι οὐκ ἔγνωσαν τὸν πατέρα οὐδὲ ἐμέ.
translation: They will do these things because they have not known the Father nor me. (John 16:3)

text: εἰ δέ τις ἀγαπᾷ τὸν θεόν, οὗτος ἔγνωσται ὑπ’ αὐτοῦ.
translation: But anyone who loves God is known by him. (1 Corinthians 8:3)

text: Ἔγνω κύριος τοὺς ὄντας αὐτοῦ, καί·
translation: However, God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.” (2 Timothy 2:19)

text: ὅθεν γινώσκομεν ὅτι ἐσχάτη ὥρα ἐστίν.
translation: Little children, these are the end times, and as you heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have arisen. By this we know that it is the final hour. (1 John 2:18)

text: καὶ λέγοντες· Ἄνδρες, τί ταῦτα ποιεῖτε;
translation: “Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men of the same nature as you, and bring you good news, that you should turn from these vain things to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and all that is in them; (Acts 14:15)

text: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἀνὴρ ἐκ γυναικός, ἀλλὰ γυνὴ ἐξ ἀνδρός·
translation: For man is not from woman, but woman from man; (1 Corinthians 11:8)

text: πλὴν οὔτε γυνὴ χωρὶς ἀνδρὸς οὔτε ἀνὴρ χωρὶς γυναικὸς ἐν κυρίῳ·
translation: Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord. (1 Corinthians 11:11)

text: εἶπεν δὲ Μαριὰμ πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον· Πῶς ἔσται τοῦτο, ἐπεὶ ἄνδρα οὐ γινώσκω;
translation: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, seeing I am a virgin?” (Luke 1:34)

text: Ἰακὼβ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ἰωσὴφ τὸν ἄνδρα Μαρίας, ἐξ ἧς ἐγεννήθη Ἰησοῦς ὁ λεγόμενος χριστός.
translation: Jacob became the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ. (Matthew 1:16)

text: ἐξ ἡμῶν ἐξῆλθαν, ἀλλ’ οὐκ ἦσαν ἐξ ἡμῶν·
translation: They went out from us, but they didn’t belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have continued with us. But they left, that they might be revealed that none of them belong to us. (1 John 2:19)

text: νῦν οὖν ἐξελθόντες πορεύεσθε ἐν εἰρήνῃ.
translation: The jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, “The magistrates have sent to let you go; now therefore come out and go in peace.” (Acts 16:36)

text: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν αὐτῇ τῇ ὥρᾳ.
translation: She was doing this for many days. But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” It came out that very hour. (Acts 16:18)

text: ἀλλὰ τί ἐξήλθατε ἰδεῖν;
translation: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed and live delicately are in kings’ courts. (Luke 7:25)

text: ὠργίσθη δὲ καὶ οὐκ ἤθελεν εἰσελθεῖν. ὁ δὲ πατὴρ αὐτοῦ ἐξελθὼν παρεκάλει αὐτόν.
translation: But he was angry and would not go in. Therefore his father came out and begged him. (Luke 15:28)

text: Παῦλος δὲ ἐπιλεξάμενος Σιλᾶν ἐξῆλθεν παραδοθεὶς τῇ χάριτι τοῦ κυρίου ὑπὸ τῶν ἀδελφῶν,
translation: but Paul chose Silas and went out, being commended by the brothers to the grace of God. (Acts 15:40)

text: καὶ ἐσώθη ἡ γυνὴ ἀπὸ τῆς ὥρας ἐκείνης.
translation: But Jesus, turning around and seeing her, said, “Daughter, cheer up! Your faith has made you well.” And the woman was made well from that hour. (Matthew 9:22)

text: λέγει οὖν αὐτῷ ἡ γυνὴ ἡ Σαμαρῖτις·
translation: The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, “How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.) (John 4:9)

text: Βλέπεις ταύτην τὴν γυναῖκα;
translation: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head. (Luke 7:44)

text: ἑπτὰ ἀδελφοὶ ἦσαν· καὶ ὁ πρῶτος ἔλαβεν γυναῖκα, καὶ ἀποθνῄσκων οὐκ ἀφῆκεν σπέρμα·
translation: There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring. (Mark 12:20)

text: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς τὴν γυναῖκα· Ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε· πορεύου εἰς εἰρήνην.
translation: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.” (Luke 7:50)

text: Καὶ ἐπὶ τούτῳ ἦλθαν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἐθαύμαζον ὅτι μετὰ γυναικὸς ἐλάλει·
translation: Just then, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, “What are you looking for?” or, “Why do you speak with her?” (John 4:27)

text: ἀπεκρίθη Νικόδημος καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Πῶς δύναται ταῦτα γενέσθαι;
translation: Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?” (John 3:9)

text: Δυνάμεθα. ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς·
translation: They said to him, “We are able.” Jesus said to them, “You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with; (Mark 10:39)

text: πῶς δύνασαι λέγειν τῷ ἀδελφῷ σου·
translation: Or how can you tell your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye. (Luke 6:42)

text: εἰ μὴ ἦν οὗτος παρὰ θεοῦ, οὐκ ἠδύνατο ποιεῖν οὐδέν.
translation: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.” (John 9:33)

text: οὐ δυνάμεθα γὰρ ἡμεῖς ἃ εἴδαμεν καὶ ἠκούσαμεν μὴ λαλεῖν.
translation: for we can’t help telling the things which we saw and heard.” (Acts 4:20)

text: Πιστεύετε ὅτι δύναμαι τοῦτο ποιῆσαι;
translation: When he had come into the house, the blind men came to him. Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They told him, “Yes, Lord.” (Matthew 9:28)

text: πλὴν οὐχ ὡς ἐγὼ θέλω ἀλλ’ ὡς σύ.
translation: He went forward a little, fell on his face, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not what I desire, but what you desire.” (Matthew 26:39)

text: ἀλλ’ οὐ τί ἐγὼ θέλω ἀλλὰ τί σύ.
translation: He said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.” (Mark 14:36)

text: Ἐὰν αὐτὸν θέλω μένειν ἕως ἔρχομαι, τί πρὸς σέ;
translation: This saying therefore went out among the brothers that this disciple wouldn’t die. Yet Jesus didn’t say to him that he wouldn’t die, but, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?” (John 21:23)

text: Τί ἂν θέλοι ὁ σπερμολόγος οὗτος λέγειν;
translation: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also were conversing with him. Some said, “What does this babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be advocating foreign deities,” because he preached Jesus and the resurrection. (Acts 17:18)

text: καὶ ὃς ἂν θέλῃ ἐν ὑμῖν εἶναι πρῶτος ἔσται ὑμῶν δοῦλος·
translation: Whoever desires to be first among you shall be your bondservant, (Matthew 20:27)

text: καὶ ὃς ἂν θέλῃ ἐν ὑμῖν εἶναι πρῶτος, ἔσται πάντων δοῦλος·
translation: Whoever of you wants to become first among you shall be bondservant of all. (Mark 10:44)

text: ἔλεγον οὖν τῷ Πιλάτῳ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς τῶν Ἰουδαίων·
translation: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.”’” (John 19:21)

text: συνεπέθεντο δὲ καὶ οἱ Ἰουδαῖοι φάσκοντες ταῦτα οὕτως ἔχειν.
translation: The Jews also joined in the attack, affirming that these things were so. (Acts 24:9)

text: Σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν Ἰουδαίων;
translation: Now Jesus stood before the governor; and the governor asked him, saying, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said to him, “So you say.” (Matthew 27:11)

text: εἶπον οὖν οἱ Ἰουδαῖοι πρὸς ἑαυτούς·
translation: The Jews therefore said among themselves, “Where will this man go that we won’t find him? Will he go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks? (John 7:35)

text: Σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν Ἰουδαίων;
translation: Pilate therefore entered again into the Praetorium, called Jesus, and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” (John 18:33)

text: καὶ λέγοντες· Εἰ σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν Ἰουδαίων, σῶσον σεαυτόν.
translation: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!” (Luke 23:37)

text: ὑμεῖς γὰρ οὐκ εἰσέρχεσθε, οὐδὲ τοὺς εἰσερχομένους ἀφίετε εἰσελθεῖν.
translation: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows’ houses, and as a pretense you make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation. (Matthew 23:13)

text: εἰσελθοῦσαι δὲ οὐχ εὗρον τὸ σῶμα.
translation: They entered in, and didn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body. (Luke 24:3)

text: καὶ εἰσῆλθεν τοῦ μεῖναι σὺν αὐτοῖς.
translation: They urged him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over.” He went in to stay with them. (Luke 24:29)

text: Καὶ εἰσελθὼν διήρχετο τὴν Ἰεριχώ.
translation: He entered and was passing through Jericho. (Luke 19:1)

text: Καὶ ὅτε εἰσῆλθεν εἰς οἶκον ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου, ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ τὴν παραβολήν.
translation: When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable. (Mark 7:17)

text: ἀλλὰ ἀνάστηθι καὶ εἴσελθε εἰς τὴν πόλιν, καὶ λαληθήσεταί σοι ὅ τί σε δεῖ ποιεῖν.
translation: But rise up and enter into the city, then you will be told what you must do.” (Acts 9:6)

text: οὗτος γάρ ἐστιν ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται.
translation: Therefore, whatever you desire for men to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets. (Matthew 7:12)

text: Τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν; ὁ νόμος ἁμαρτία;
translation: What shall we say then? Is the law sin? May it never be! However, I wouldn’t have known sin except through the law. For I wouldn’t have known coveting unless the law had said, “You shall not covet.” (Romans 7:7)

text: ἀλλὰ ὁ ὄχλος οὗτος ὁ μὴ γινώσκων τὸν νόμον ἐπάρατοί εἰσιν.
translation: But this multitude that doesn’t know the law is cursed.” (John 7:49)

text: ἐγὼ γὰρ διὰ νόμου νόμῳ ἀπέθανον ἵνα θεῷ ζήσω· Χριστῷ συνεσταύρωμαι·
translation: For I through the law died to the law, that I might live to God. (Galatians 2:19)

text: Λέγετέ μοι, οἱ ὑπὸ νόμον θέλοντες εἶναι, τὸν νόμον οὐκ ἀκούετε;
translation: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law? (Galatians 4:21)

text: Εἰ δὲ σὺ Ἰουδαῖος ἐπονομάζῃ καὶ ἐπαναπαύῃ νόμῳ καὶ καυχᾶσαι ἐν θεῷ
translation: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, rest on the law, glory in God, (Romans 2:17)

text: Καὶ τῷ ἀγγέλῳ τῆς ἐν Θυατείροις ἐκκλησίας γράψον·
translation: “To the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write: “The Son of God, who has his eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet are like burnished brass, says these things: (Revelation 2:18)

text: Καὶ τῷ ἀγγέλῳ τῆς ἐν Σάρδεσιν ἐκκλησίας γράψον·
translation: “And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: “He who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars says these things: “I know your works, that you have a reputation of being alive, but you are dead. (Revelation 3:1)

text: Καὶ τῷ ἀγγέλῳ τῆς ἐν Φιλαδελφείᾳ ἐκκλησίας γράψον·
translation: “To the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write: “He who is holy, he who is true, he who has the key of David, he who opens and no one can shut, and who shuts and no one opens, says these things: (Revelation 3:7)

text: Καὶ τῷ ἀγγέλῳ τῆς ἐν Λαοδικείᾳ ἐκκλησίας γράψον·
translation: “To the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: “The Amen, the Faithful and True Witness, the Beginning of God’s creation, says these things: (Revelation 3:14)

text: Τῷ ἀγγέλῳ τῆς ἐν Ἐφέσῳ ἐκκλησίας γράψον·
translation: “To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write: “He who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks among the seven golden lamp stands says these things: (Revelation 2:1)

text: Τί οὖν ἐστιν τὸ γεγραμμένον τοῦτο·
translation: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone’? (Luke 20:17)

text: Μὴ ἀγαπᾶτε τὸν κόσμον μηδὲ τὰ ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ.
translation: Don’t love the world or the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the Father’s love isn’t in him. (1 John 2:15)

text: διὰ τοῦτο ὁ κόσμος οὐ γινώσκει ἡμᾶς ὅτι οὐκ ἔγνω αὐτόν.
translation: See how great a love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God! For this cause the world doesn’t know us, because it didn’t know him. (1 John 3:1)

text: ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου οὐκ εἰσὶν καθὼς ἐγὼ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου.
translation: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world. (John 17:16)

text: καθὼς ἐμὲ ἀπέστειλας εἰς τὸν κόσμον, κἀγὼ ἀπέστειλα αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸν κόσμον·
translation: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world. (John 17:18)

text: ὑμεῖς ἐκ τούτου τοῦ κόσμου ἐστέ, ἐγὼ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου τούτου.
translation: He said to them, “You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world. (John 8:23)

text: ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ τὸν κόσμον, οὐκ ἔστιν ἡ ἀγάπη τοῦ πατρὸς ἐν αὐτῷ·
translation: Don’t love the world or the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the Father’s love isn’t in him. (1 John 2:15)

text: ἀπελθόντες δὲ οἱ ἀπεσταλμένοι εὗρον καθὼς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς.
translation: Those who were sent went away and found things just as he had told them. (Luke 19:32)

text: εὑρίσκει Φίλιππος τὸν Ναθαναὴλ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ·
translation: Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him of whom Moses in the law and also the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.” (John 1:45)

text: καὶ εὗρον αὐτὸν καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ὅτι Πάντες ζητοῦσίν σε.
translation: They found him and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.” (Mark 1:37)

text: Τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν εὑρηκέναι Ἀβραὰμ τὸν προπάτορα ἡμῶν κατὰ σάρκα;
translation: What then will we say that Abraham, our forefather, has found according to the flesh? (Romans 4:1)

text: μακάριος ὁ δοῦλος ἐκεῖνος ὃν ἐλθὼν ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εὑρήσει οὕτως ποιοῦντα·
translation: Blessed is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes. (Matthew 24:46)

text: μακάριος ὁ δοῦλος ἐκεῖνος, ὃν ἐλθὼν ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εὑρήσει ποιοῦντα οὕτως·
translation: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes. (Luke 12:43)

text: ὁ πατὴρ ἀγαπᾷ τὸν υἱόν, καὶ πάντα δέδωκεν ἐν τῇ χειρὶ αὐτοῦ.
translation: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand. (John 3:35)

text: γράψαντες διὰ χειρὸς αὐτῶν·
translation: They wrote these things by their hand: “The apostles, the elders, and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: greetings. (Acts 15:23)

text: λέγων· Δότε κἀμοὶ τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην ἵνα ᾧ ἐὰν ἐπιθῶ τὰς χεῖρας λαμβάνῃ πνεῦμα ἅγιον.
translation: saying, “Give me also this power, that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.” (Acts 8:19)

text: ἀπῆλθεν δὲ Ἁνανίας καὶ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν, καὶ ἐπιθεὶς ἐπ’ αὐτὸν τὰς χεῖρας εἶπεν·
translation: Ananias departed and entered into the house. Laying his hands on him, he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord, who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.” (Acts 9:17)

text: αὐτοὶ γινώσκετε ὅτι ταῖς χρείαις μου καὶ τοῖς οὖσι μετ’ ἐμοῦ ὑπηρέτησαν αἱ χεῖρες αὗται.
translation: You yourselves know that these hands served my necessities, and those who were with me. (Acts 20:34)

text: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν· Ὁ ἐμβάψας μετ’ ἐμοῦ τὴν χεῖρα ἐν τῷ τρυβλίῳ οὗτός με παραδώσει·
translation: He answered, “He who dipped his hand with me in the dish will betray me. (Matthew 26:23)

text: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ ἄγγελος·
translation: But the angel said to him, “Don’t be afraid, Zacharias, because your request has been heard. Your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John. (Luke 1:13)

text: καὶ εἶπεν Ζαχαρίας πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον·
translation: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.” (Luke 1:18)

text: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news. (Luke 1:19)

text: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν αὐτῇ·
translation: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God. (Luke 1:35)

text: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ ἄγγελος πρὸς αὐτόν·
translation: The angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” He did so. He said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.” (Acts 12:8)

text: καὶ ἀπῆλθα πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον λέγων αὐτῷ δοῦναί μοι τὸ βιβλαρίδιον.
translation: I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little book. He said to me, “Take it and eat it. It will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey.” (Revelation 10:9)

text: ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ εἶπεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς τοῖς ὄχλοις·
translation: In that hour Jesus said to the multitudes, “Have you come out as against a robber with swords and clubs to seize me? I sat daily in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. (Matthew 26:55)

text: Τίνα με οἱ ὄχλοι λέγουσιν εἶναι;
translation: As he was praying alone, the disciples were near him, and he asked them, “Who do the multitudes say that I am?” (Luke 9:18)

text: Οὔ, ἀλλὰ πλανᾷ τὸν ὄχλον.
translation: There was much murmuring among the multitudes concerning him. Some said, “He is a good man.” Others said, “Not so, but he leads the multitude astray.” (John 7:12)

text: ἀπεκρίθη οὖν αὐτῷ ὁ ὄχλος·
translation: The multitude answered him, “We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever. How do you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?” (John 12:34)

text: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν μετ’ αὐτοῦ. Καὶ ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ ὄχλος πολύς, καὶ συνέθλιβον αὐτόν.
translation: He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides. (Mark 5:24)

text: οἱ δὲ ὄχλοι ἔλεγον· Οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ προφήτης Ἰησοῦς ὁ ἀπὸ Ναζαρὲθ τῆς Γαλιλαίας.
translation: The multitudes said, “This is the prophet, Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.” (Matthew 21:11)

text: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῇ· Ἀφέωνταί σου αἱ ἁμαρτίαι.
translation: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.” (Luke 7:48)

text: εἶπον οὖν ὑμῖν ὅτι ἀποθανεῖσθε ἐν ταῖς ἁμαρτίαις ὑμῶν·
translation: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.” (John 8:24)

text: εἰδότι οὖν καλὸν ποιεῖν καὶ μὴ ποιοῦντι, ἁμαρτία αὐτῷ ἐστιν.
translation: To him therefore who knows to do good and doesn’t do it, to him it is sin. (James 4:17)

text: πᾶσα ἀδικία ἁμαρτία ἐστίν, καὶ ἔστιν ἁμαρτία οὐ πρὸς θάνατον.
translation: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is sin not leading to death. (1 John 5:17)

text: καὶ ἰδὼν τὴν πίστιν αὐτῶν εἶπεν· Ἄνθρωπε, ἀφέωνταί σοι αἱ ἁμαρτίαι σου.
translation: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.” (Luke 5:20)

text: ἐὰν γὰρ μὴ πιστεύσητε ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι, ἀποθανεῖσθε ἐν ταῖς ἁμαρτίαις ὑμῶν.
translation: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.” (John 8:24)

text: εἰ οὐ ποιῶ τὰ ἔργα τοῦ πατρός μου, μὴ πιστεύετέ μοι·
translation: If I don’t do the works of my Father, don’t believe me. (John 10:37)

text: οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις, ἐὰν μὴ ἔχῃ ἔργα, νεκρά ἐστιν καθ’ ἑαυτήν.
translation: Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself. (James 2:17)

text: ἀλλὰ τοῦτο ἔχεις ὅτι μισεῖς τὰ ἔργα τῶν Νικολαϊτῶν, ἃ κἀγὼ μισῶ.
translation: But this you have, that you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate. (Revelation 2:6)

text: εἰ τὰ ἔργα μὴ ἐποίησα ἐν αὐτοῖς ἃ οὐδεὶς ἄλλος ἐποίησεν, ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ εἴχοσαν·
translation: If I hadn’t done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn’t have had sin. But now they have seen and also hated both me and my Father. (John 15:24)

text: τὰ ἔργα ἃ ἐγὼ ποιῶ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ πατρός μου ταῦτα μαρτυρεῖ περὶ ἐμοῦ·
translation: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you don’t believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name, these testify about me. (John 10:25)

text: καθάπερ καὶ Δαυὶδ λέγει τὸν μακαρισμὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ᾧ ὁ θεὸς λογίζεται δικαιοσύνην χωρὶς ἔργων·
translation: Even as David also pronounces blessing on the man to whom God counts righteousness apart from works: (Romans 4:6)

text: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων· ἀμήν.
translation: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen. (Galatians 1:5)

text: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων, ἀμήν.
translation: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen. (2 Timothy 4:18)

text: οὔτε ζητοῦντες ἐξ ἀνθρώπων δόξαν, οὔτε ἀφ’ ὑμῶν οὔτε ἀπ’ ἄλλων,
translation: nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ. (1 Thessalonians 2:6)

text: ἐγὼ δὲ οὐ ζητῶ τὴν δόξαν μου· ἔστιν ὁ ζητῶν καὶ κρίνων.
translation: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges. (John 8:50)

text: Δὸς δόξαν τῷ θεῷ·
translation: So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.” (John 9:24)

text: ταῦτα εἶπεν Ἠσαΐας ὅτι εἶδεν τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἐλάλησεν περὶ αὐτοῦ.
translation: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory, and spoke of him. (John 12:41)

text: ἦν δὲ ὁ Φίλιππος ἀπὸ Βηθσαϊδά, ἐκ τῆς πόλεως Ἀνδρέου καὶ Πέτρου.
translation: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter. (John 1:44)

text: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν· Ὑπάγετε εἰς τὴν πόλιν πρὸς τὸν δεῖνα καὶ εἴπατε αὐτῷ·
translation: He said, “Go into the city to a certain person, and tell him, ‘The Teacher says, “My time is at hand. I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.”’” (Matthew 26:18)

text: ἀφῆκεν οὖν τὴν ὑδρίαν αὐτῆς ἡ γυνὴ καὶ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ λέγει τοῖς ἀνθρώποις·
translation: So the woman left her water pot, went away into the city, and said to the people, (John 4:28)

text: Καὶ ὡς ἐτέλεσαν πάντα τὰ κατὰ τὸν νόμον κυρίου, ἐπέστρεψαν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν εἰς πόλιν ἑαυτῶν Ναζαρέθ.
translation: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth. (Luke 2:39)

text: Φίλιππος δὲ εὑρέθη εἰς Ἄζωτον, καὶ διερχόμενος εὐηγγελίζετο τὰς πόλεις πάσας ἕως τοῦ ἐλθεῖν αὐτὸν εἰς Καισάρειαν.
translation: But Philip was found at Azotus. Passing through, he preached the Good News to all the cities until he came to Caesarea. (Acts 8:40)

text: ἀμὴν γὰρ λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐ μὴ τελέσητε τὰς πόλεις τοῦ Ἰσραὴλ ἕως ἂν ἔλθῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
translation: But when they persecute you in this city, flee into the next, for most certainly I tell you, you will not have gone through the cities of Israel until the Son of Man has come. (Matthew 10:23)

text: τότε ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Ἐγερθήσεται ἔθνος ἐπ’ ἔθνος καὶ βασιλεία ἐπὶ βασιλείαν,
translation: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. (Luke 21:10)

text: καὶ ἔλεγεν· Ἰησοῦ, μνήσθητί μου ὅταν ἔλθῃς ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ σου.
translation: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.” (Luke 23:42)

text: λέγω γὰρ ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ φάγω αὐτὸ ἕως ὅτου πληρωθῇ ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ.
translation: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.” (Luke 22:16)

text: Τότε οἱ δίκαιοι ἐκλάμψουσιν ὡς ὁ ἥλιος ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτῶν. ὁ ἔχων ὦτα ἀκουέτω.
translation: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the Kingdom of their Father. He who has ears to hear, let him hear. (Matthew 13:43)

text: κηρύσσων τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ διδάσκων τὰ περὶ τοῦ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ μετὰ πάσης παρρησίας ἀκωλύτως.
translation: preaching God’s Kingdom and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, without hindrance. (Acts 28:31)

text: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ὃς ἂν μὴ δέξηται τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ὡς παιδίον, οὐ μὴ εἰσέλθῃ εἰς αὐτήν.
translation: Most certainly I tell you, whoever will not receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.” (Mark 10:15)

text: οὓς καὶ ἐκάλεσεν ἡμᾶς οὐ μόνον ἐξ Ἰουδαίων ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐξ ἐθνῶν;—
translation: us, whom he also called, not from the Jews only, but also from the Gentiles? (Romans 9:24)

text: ὃς παρ’ ἐλπίδα ἐπ’ ἐλπίδι ἐπίστευσεν εἰς τὸ γενέσθαι αὐτὸν πατέρα πολλῶν ἐθνῶν κατὰ τὸ εἰρημένον·
translation: Against hope, Abraham in hope believed, to the end that he might become a father of many nations, according to that which had been spoken, “So will your offspring be.” (Romans 4:18)

text: καὶ ἀπὸ τότε ἐζήτει εὐκαιρίαν ἵνα αὐτὸν παραδῷ.
translation: From that time he sought opportunity to betray him. (Matthew 26:16)

text: τότε ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῇ·
translation: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour. (Matthew 15:28)

text: τότε ὁ Παῦλος πρὸς αὐτὸν εἶπεν·
translation: Then Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! Do you sit to judge me according to the law, and command me to be struck contrary to the law?” (Acts 23:3)

text: τότε ἀποκριθήσονται καὶ αὐτοὶ λέγοντες·
translation: “Then they will also answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and didn’t help you?’ (Matthew 25:44)

text: Τότε λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς·
translation: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’ (Matthew 26:31)

text: τότε λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς·
translation: Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place, for all those who take the sword will die by the sword. (Matthew 26:52)

text: Δότε αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς φαγεῖν.
translation: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give them something to eat?” (Mark 6:37)

text: Δότε αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς φαγεῖν.
translation: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people.” (Luke 9:13)

text: καὶ ἐσθιόντων αὐτῶν εἶπεν· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι εἷς ἐξ ὑμῶν παραδώσει με.
translation: As they were eating, he said, “Most certainly I tell you that one of you will betray me.” (Matthew 26:21)

text: Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι εἷς ἐξ ὑμῶν παραδώσει με ὁ ἐσθίων μετ’ ἐμοῦ.
translation: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me—he who eats with me.” (Mark 14:18)

text: Εἴτε οὖν ἐσθίετε εἴτε πίνετε εἴτε τι ποιεῖτε, πάντα εἰς δόξαν θεοῦ ποιεῖτε.
translation: Whether therefore you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God. (1 Corinthians 10:31)

text: ἔλεγον οὖν αὐτῷ οἱ ἄλλοι μαθηταί·
translation: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.” (John 20:25)

text: Πορεύθητι, καὶ πορεύεται, καὶ ἄλλῳ·
translation: For I am also a man under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and tell another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and tell my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” (Matthew 8:9)

text: πάλιν ἀπέστειλεν ἄλλους δούλους λέγων·
translation: Again he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “Behold, I have prepared my dinner. My cattle and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast!”’ (Matthew 22:4)

text: Πορεύθητι, καὶ πορεύεται, καὶ ἄλλῳ·
translation: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” (Luke 7:8)

text: Ἄλλην παραβολὴν ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς·
translation: He spoke another parable to them. “The Kingdom of Heaven is like yeast which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, until it was all leavened.” (Matthew 13:33)

text: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς· Ἀπὸ σεαυτοῦ σὺ τοῦτο λέγεις ἢ ἄλλοι εἶπόν σοι περὶ ἐμοῦ;
translation: Jesus answered him, “Do you say this by yourself, or did others tell you about me?” (John 18:34)

text: ἀπέστειλεν οὖν αὐτὸν ὁ Ἅννας δεδεμένον πρὸς Καϊάφαν τὸν ἀρχιερέα.
translation: Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas, the high priest. (John 18:24)

text: καὶ ὧδε ἔχει ἐξουσίαν παρὰ τῶν ἀρχιερέων δῆσαι πάντας τοὺς ἐπικαλουμένους τὸ ὄνομά σου.
translation: Here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.” (Acts 9:14)

text: τὸ γὰρ τί προσευξώμεθα καθὸ δεῖ οὐκ οἴδαμεν, ἀλλὰ αὐτὸ τὸ πνεῦμα ὑπερεντυγχάνει στεναγμοῖς ἀλαλήτοις,
translation: In the same way, the Spirit also helps our weaknesses, for we don’t know how to pray as we ought. But the Spirit himself makes intercession for us with groanings which can’t be uttered. (Romans 8:26)

text: καὶ ἐπηρώτησαν αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ λέγοντες· Τί οὖν οἱ γραμματεῖς λέγουσιν ὅτι Ἠλίαν δεῖ ἐλθεῖν πρῶτον;
translation: His disciples asked him, saying, “Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?” (Matthew 17:10)

text: ἡμᾶς δεῖ ἐργάζεσθαι τὰ ἔργα τοῦ πέμψαντός με ἕως ἡμέρα ἐστίν· ἔρχεται νὺξ ὅτε οὐδεὶς δύναται ἐργάζεσθαι.
translation: I must work the works of him who sent me while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work. (John 9:4)

text: σταθεὶς δὲ Ζακχαῖος εἶπεν πρὸς τὸν κύριον·
translation: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.” (Luke 19:8)

text: Ἦν δὲ Σίμων Πέτρος ἑστὼς καὶ θερμαινόμενος.
translation: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, “You aren’t also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.” (John 18:25)

text: ἦν δὲ καὶ ὁ Πέτρος μετ’ αὐτῶν ἑστὼς καὶ θερμαινόμενος.
translation: Now the servants and the officers were standing there, having made a fire of coals, for it was cold. They were warming themselves. Peter was with them, standing and warming himself. (John 18:18)

text: ἐζήτουν οὖν τὸν Ἰησοῦν καὶ ἔλεγον μετ’ ἀλλήλων ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἑστηκότες·
translation: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke with one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think—that he isn’t coming to the feast at all?” (John 11:56)

text: Ἤγαγεν δὲ αὐτὸν εἰς Ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ ἔστησεν ἐπὶ τὸ πτερύγιον τοῦ ἱεροῦ, καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: He led him to Jerusalem and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here, (Luke 4:9)

text: Ἐν δὲ τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ μεγάλῃ τῆς ἑορτῆς εἱστήκει ὁ Ἰησοῦς, καὶ ἔκραξεν λέγων·
translation: Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink! (John 7:37)

text: λέγουσιν· Ὁ πρῶτος. λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς·
translation: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said to him, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom before you. (Matthew 21:31)

text: ἡμεῖς ἀγαπῶμεν, ὅτι αὐτὸς πρῶτος ἠγάπησεν ἡμᾶς.
translation: We love him, because he first loved us. (1 John 4:19)

text: Ὁ ὀπίσω μου ἐρχόμενος ἔμπροσθέν μου γέγονεν, ὅτι πρῶτός μου ἦν·)
translation: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’” (John 1:15)

text: ἀλλὰ ἔχω κατὰ σοῦ ὅτι τὴν ἀγάπην σου τὴν πρώτην ἀφῆκες.
translation: But I have this against you, that you left your first love. (Revelation 2:4)

text: ὁ πρῶτος εἶπεν αὐτῷ·
translation: They all as one began to make excuses. “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’ (Luke 14:18)

text: καὶ ἰδοὺ εἰσὶν ἔσχατοι οἳ ἔσονται πρῶτοι, καὶ εἰσὶν πρῶτοι οἳ ἔσονται ἔσχατοι.
translation: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.” (Luke 13:30)

text: λέγων· Πορεύθητι πρὸς τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον καὶ εἰπόν·
translation: saying, ‘Go to this people and say, in hearing, you will hear, but will in no way understand. In seeing, you will see, but will in no way perceive. (Acts 28:26)

text: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ἐπορεύετο σὺν αὐτοῖς.
translation: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof. (Luke 7:6)

text: Πορεύου· ὁ υἱός σου ζῇ.
translation: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your son lives.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way. (John 4:50)

text: μὴ πορευθῆτε ὀπίσω αὐτῶν.
translation: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them. (Luke 21:8)

text: Σοὶ λέγω, ἔγειρε καὶ ἄρας τὸ κλινίδιόν σου πορεύου εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου.
translation: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins,” he said to the paralyzed man, “I tell you, arise, take up your cot, and go to your house.” (Luke 5:24)

text: Καὶ πορευομένων αὐτῶν ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ εἶπέν τις πρὸς αὐτόν· Ἀκολουθήσω σοι ὅπου ἐὰν ἀπέρχῃ.
translation: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.” (Luke 9:57)

text: μακάριοι οἱ εἰρηνοποιοί, ὅτι αὐτοὶ υἱοὶ θεοῦ κληθήσονται.
translation: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God. (Matthew 5:9)

text: καὶ ἐλθὼν ὁ σὲ καὶ αὐτὸν καλέσας ἐρεῖ σοι·
translation: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place. (Luke 14:9)

text: ἡ πεισμονὴ οὐκ ἐκ τοῦ καλοῦντος ὑμᾶς.
translation: This persuasion is not from him who calls you. (Galatians 5:8)

text: Ἐξ Αἰγύπτου ἐκάλεσα τὸν υἱόν μου.
translation: and was there until the death of Herod, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called my son.” (Matthew 2:15)

text: εἰ οὖν Δαυὶδ καλεῖ αὐτὸν κύριον, πῶς υἱὸς αὐτοῦ ἐστιν;
translation: “If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?” (Matthew 22:45)

text: Ἔλεγεν δὲ καὶ τῷ κεκληκότι αὐτόν·
translation: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back. (Luke 14:12)

text: ὥστε οὐκέτι εἰσὶν δύο ἀλλὰ σὰρξ μία. ὃ οὖν ὁ θεὸς συνέζευξεν ἄνθρωπος μὴ χωριζέτω.
translation: So that they are no more two, but one flesh. What therefore God has joined together, don’t let man tear apart.” (Matthew 19:6)

text: τὸ γεγεννημένον ἐκ τῆς σαρκὸς σάρξ ἐστιν, καὶ τὸ γεγεννημένον ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος πνεῦμά ἐστιν.
translation: That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit. (John 3:6)

text: καθὼς ἔδωκας αὐτῷ ἐξουσίαν πάσης σαρκός, ἵνα πᾶν ὃ δέδωκας αὐτῷ δώσῃ αὐτοῖς ζωὴν αἰώνιον.
translation: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, so he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him. (John 17:2)

text: ἢ ἃ βουλεύομαι κατὰ σάρκα βουλεύομαι, ἵνα ᾖ παρ’ ἐμοὶ τὸ Ναὶ ναὶ καὶ τὸ Οὒ οὔ;
translation: When I therefore planned this, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I plan, do I plan according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?” (2 Corinthians 1:17)

text: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ καὶ νεκροῖς εὐηγγελίσθη ἵνα κριθῶσι μὲν κατὰ ἀνθρώπους σαρκὶ ζῶσι δὲ κατὰ θεὸν πνεύματι.
translation: For to this end the Good News was preached even to the dead, that they might be judged indeed as men in the flesh, but live as to God in the spirit. (1 Peter 4:6)

text: οὐδεὶς γάρ ποτε τὴν ἑαυτοῦ σάρκα ἐμίσησεν, ἀλλὰ ἐκτρέφει καὶ θάλπει αὐτήν, καθὼς καὶ ὁ Χριστὸς τὴν ἐκκλησίαν,
translation: For no man ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, even as the Lord also does the assembly, (Ephesians 5:29)

text: Οὐδὲν ἄρα νῦν κατάκριμα τοῖς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ·
translation: There is therefore now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus, who don’t walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit. (Romans 8:1)

text: καὶ νῦν εἴρηκα ὑμῖν πρὶν γενέσθαι, ἵνα ὅταν γένηται πιστεύσητε.
translation: Now I have told you before it happens so that when it happens, you may believe. (John 14:29)

text: νῦν δὲ καὶ ἑωράκασιν καὶ μεμισήκασιν καὶ ἐμὲ καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου.
translation: If I hadn’t done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn’t have had sin. But now they have seen and also hated both me and my Father. (John 15:24)

text: καὶ νῦν οἶδα ὅτι ὅσα ἂν αἰτήσῃ τὸν θεὸν δώσει σοι ὁ θεός.
translation: Even now I know that whatever you ask of God, God will give you.” (John 11:22)

text: καὶ τὰ νῦν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀπόστητε ἀπὸ τῶν ἀνθρώπων τούτων καὶ ἄφετε αὐτούς·
translation: Now I tell you, withdraw from these men and leave them alone. For if this counsel or this work is of men, it will be overthrown. (Acts 5:38)

text: πῶς δὲ νῦν βλέπει οὐκ οἴδαμεν, ἢ τίς ἤνοιξεν αὐτοῦ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ἡμεῖς οὐκ οἴδαμεν·
translation: but how he now sees, we don’t know; or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself.” (John 9:21)

text: Ἀβραὰμ ἀπέθανεν καὶ οἱ προφῆται, καὶ σὺ λέγεις·
translation: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, as did the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’ (John 8:52)

text: ἀλλὰ τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ εἰρημένον διὰ τοῦ προφήτου Ἰωήλ·
translation: But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel: (Acts 2:16)

text: ἵνα πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν ὑπὸ κυρίου διὰ τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος·
translation: and was there until the death of Herod, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called my son.” (Matthew 2:15)

text: οὗτος γάρ ἐστιν ὁ ῥηθεὶς διὰ Ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος·
translation: For this is he who was spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, make the way of the Lord ready! Make his paths straight!” (Matthew 3:3)

text: λέγει δὲ Ἀβραάμ· Ἔχουσι Μωϋσέα καὶ τοὺς προφήτας· ἀκουσάτωσαν αὐτῶν.
translation: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’ (Luke 16:29)

text: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ὅτι Προφήτης ἐστίν.
translation: Therefore they asked the blind man again, “What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.” (John 9:17)

text: εἰ γὰρ νεκροὶ οὐκ ἐγείρονται, οὐδὲ Χριστὸς ἐγήγερται·
translation: For if the dead aren’t raised, neither has Christ been raised. (1 Corinthians 15:16)

text: καὶ ἐγερθεὶς ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ.
translation: He arose and departed to his house. (Matthew 9:7)

text: ὁ δὲ θεὸς ἤγειρεν αὐτὸν ἐκ νεκρῶν·
translation: But God raised him from the dead, (Acts 13:30)

text: καὶ ἐγείρουσιν αὐτὸν καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ·
translation: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke him up and asked him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?” (Mark 4:38)

text: Ἀλλὰ ἐρεῖ τις· Πῶς ἐγείρονται οἱ νεκροί, ποίῳ δὲ σώματι ἔρχονται;
translation: But someone will say, “How are the dead raised?” and, “With what kind of body do they come?” (1 Corinthians 15:35)

text: ὁ δὲ Πέτρος ἤγειρεν αὐτὸν λέγων· Ἀνάστηθι· καὶ ἐγὼ αὐτὸς ἄνθρωπός εἰμι.
translation: But Peter raised him up, saying, “Stand up! I myself am also a man.” (Acts 10:26)

text: καὶ οὐκ ἀφῆκεν αὐτόν, ἀλλὰ λέγει αὐτῷ·
translation: He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you and how he had mercy on you.” (Mark 5:19)

text: ἀφῆκεν τὴν Ἰουδαίαν καὶ ἀπῆλθεν πάλιν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν.
translation: he left Judea and departed into Galilee. (John 4:3)

text: Οὐκ ἀφήσω ὑμᾶς ὀρφανούς, ἔρχομαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς.
translation: I will not leave you orphans. I will come to you. (John 14:18)

text: ἰδοὺ ἀφίεται ὑμῖν ὁ οἶκος ὑμῶν.
translation: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’” (Luke 13:35)

text: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν αὐτοῖς καθὼς εἶπεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς· καὶ ἀφῆκαν αὐτούς.
translation: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go. (Mark 11:6)

text: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς· Εἶπον ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι· εἰ οὖν ἐμὲ ζητεῖτε, ἄφετε τούτους ὑπάγειν·
translation: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,” (John 18:8)

text: ἀλλ’ ἐν τούτοις πᾶσιν ὑπερνικῶμεν διὰ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντος ἡμᾶς.
translation: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us. (Romans 8:37)

text: διὰ τί; ὅτι οὐκ ἀγαπῶ ὑμᾶς; ὁ θεὸς οἶδεν.
translation: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows. (2 Corinthians 11:11)

text: ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν μένει ἐν τῷ θανάτῳ.
translation: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. He who doesn’t love his brother remains in death. (1 John 3:14)

text: λέγει οὖν ὁ μαθητὴς ἐκεῖνος ὃν ἠγάπα ὁ Ἰησοῦς τῷ Πέτρῳ·
translation: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!” So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea. (John 21:7)

text: Σίμων Ἰωάννου, ἀγαπᾷς με;
translation: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.” (John 21:16)

text: Ὅτι αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἀγγελία ἣν ἠκούσατε ἀπ’ ἀρχῆς, ἵνα ἀγαπῶμεν ἀλλήλους·
translation: For this is the message which you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another— (1 John 3:11)

text: ἦλθεν οὖν καὶ ἦρεν τὸ σῶμα αὐτοῦ.
translation: After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus’ body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body. (John 19:38)

text: καὶ μὴ φοβεῖσθε ἀπὸ τῶν ἀποκτεννόντων τὸ σῶμα τὴν δὲ ψυχὴν μὴ δυναμένων ἀποκτεῖναι·
translation: Don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul. Rather, fear him who is able to destroy both soul and body in Gehenna. (Matthew 10:28)

text: ὁ δὲ θεὸς δίδωσιν αὐτῷ σῶμα καθὼς ἠθέλησεν, καὶ ἑκάστῳ τῶν σπερμάτων ἴδιον σῶμα.
translation: But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own. (1 Corinthians 15:38)

text: ὥσπερ γὰρ τὸ σῶμα χωρὶς πνεύματος νεκρόν ἐστιν, οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις χωρὶς ἔργων νεκρά ἐστιν.
translation: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead. (James 2:26)

text: εἰ δὲ Χριστὸς ἐν ὑμῖν, τὸ μὲν σῶμα νεκρὸν διὰ ἁμαρτίαν, τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα ζωὴ διὰ δικαιοσύνην.
translation: If Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the spirit is alive because of righteousness. (Romans 8:10)

text: Ὅτι οὐκ εἰμὶ χείρ, οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐκ τοῦ σώματος, οὐ παρὰ τοῦτο οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τοῦ σώματος;
translation: If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body. (1 Corinthians 12:15)

text: Λέγω οὖν, μὴ ἀπώσατο ὁ θεὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ;
translation: I ask then, did God reject his people? May it never be! For I also am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin. (Romans 11:1)

text: καὶ πᾶς ὁ λαὸς ὤρθριζεν πρὸς αὐτὸν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἀκούειν αὐτοῦ.
translation: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him. (Luke 21:38)

text: καὶ οὐχ εὕρισκον τὸ τί ποιήσωσιν, ὁ λαὸς γὰρ ἅπας ἐξεκρέματο αὐτοῦ ἀκούων.
translation: They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said. (Luke 19:48)

text: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν ἀπολέσαι καὶ οἱ πρῶτοι τοῦ λαοῦ,
translation: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him. (Luke 19:47)

text: ἔσται δὲ πᾶσα ψυχὴ ἥτις ἂν μὴ ἀκούσῃ τοῦ προφήτου ἐκείνου ἐξολεθρευθήσεται ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ.
translation: It will be that every soul that will not listen to that prophet will be utterly destroyed from among the people.’ (Acts 3:23)

text: Πορεύεσθε καὶ σταθέντες λαλεῖτε ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ τῷ λαῷ πάντα τὰ ῥήματα τῆς ζωῆς ταύτης.
translation: “Go stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.” (Acts 5:20)

text: Τί ζητεῖτε τὸν ζῶντα μετὰ τῶν νεκρῶν;
translation: Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth. The men said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead? (Luke 24:5)

text: Οὐδεὶς γὰρ ἡμῶν ἑαυτῷ ζῇ, καὶ οὐδεὶς ἑαυτῷ ἀποθνῄσκει·
translation: For none of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself. (Romans 14:7)

text: ἐμοὶ γὰρ τὸ ζῆν Χριστὸς καὶ τὸ ἀποθανεῖν κέρδος.
translation: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain. (Philippians 1:21)

text: ἐάν τε οὖν ζῶμεν ἐάν τε ἀποθνῄσκωμεν, τοῦ κυρίου ἐσμέν.
translation: For if we live, we live to the Lord. Or if we die, we die to the Lord. If therefore we live or die, we are the Lord’s. (Romans 14:8)

text: θεὸς δὲ οὐκ ἔστιν νεκρῶν ἀλλὰ ζώντων, πάντες γὰρ αὐτῷ ζῶσιν.
translation: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.” (Luke 20:38)

text: Αἶρε ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς τὸν τοιοῦτον, οὐ γὰρ καθῆκεν αὐτὸν ζῆν.
translation: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice and said, “Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn’t fit to live!” (Acts 22:22)

text: καὶ ἤκουσα φωνὴν ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ λέγουσαν·
translation: When the seven thunders sounded, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from the sky saying, “Seal up the things which the seven thunders said, and don’t write them.” (Revelation 10:4)

text: Καὶ ἤκουσα φωνῆς ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ λεγούσης·
translation: I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Write, ‘Blessed are the dead who die in the Lord from now on.’” “Yes,” says the Spirit, “that they may rest from their labors, for their works follow with them.” (Revelation 14:13)

text: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν· Οὐ δι’ ἐμὲ ἡ φωνὴ αὕτη γέγονεν ἀλλὰ δι’ ὑμᾶς.
translation: Jesus answered, “This voice hasn’t come for my sake, but for your sakes. (John 12:30)

text: Καὶ ἡ φωνὴ ἣν ἤκουσα ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, πάλιν λαλοῦσαν μετ’ ἐμοῦ καὶ λέγουσαν·
translation: The voice which I heard from heaven, again speaking with me, said, “Go, take the book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.” (Revelation 10:8)

text: πάτερ, δόξασόν σου τὸ ὄνομα. ἦλθεν οὖν φωνὴ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ· Καὶ ἐδόξασα καὶ πάλιν δοξάσω.
translation: Father, glorify your name!” Then a voice came out of the sky, saying, “I have both glorified it and will glorify it again.” (John 12:28)

text: καὶ ἰδοὺ φωνὴ ἐκ τῶν οὐρανῶν λέγουσα· Οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός, ἐν ᾧ εὐδόκησα.
translation: Behold, a voice out of the heavens said, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.” (Matthew 3:17)

text: Καὶ ἔρχονται πάλιν εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα.
translation: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him, (Mark 11:27)
